nosotros.deliePara2: Founded in 2003, DELIE is one of the world's leading manufacturers of automated storage systems. With over 1,000 installations in more than 30 countries, it combines industrial robustness, proprietary WMS/WCS software and a complete product line: stacker cranes, shuttle robots, AMR, VLM, conveyors and racks up to 40 meters.

howWeWork.metaTitle: How We Work | Turnkey ASRS Project | STOKA
howWeWork.metaDesc: 6-step methodology: consultation, engineering, installation and after-sales support. Turnkey ASRS projects in Argentina and Chile.
howWeWork.intro: A structured 6-phase process that guarantees predictable results, no surprises and local technical support at every stage.
howWeWork.readyPara: In 24 hours you get a technical response from our engineering team, with no commitment.

steps.title: Initial Consultation
steps.subtitle: Free · Response in 24 hours
steps.detail: Fill in the contact form or write to us directly. In less than 24 hours an engineer responds to understand your operation at a high level: type of warehouse or depot, SKU volume, throughput and automation goals.
logs: 30-minute meeting or call with the technical team
Initial technical questionnaire sent by email
First technology guidance with no commitment or cost

steps.title: Technical Diagnosis
steps.subtitle: On-site or remote survey
steps.detail: We analyze historical movement data, SKU catalog, demand peaks and existing warehouse layout. We measure current KPIs to establish the project baseline and identify real bottlenecks.
logs: Analysis of SKU history and daily throughput
Measurement of current KPIs: errors, times, space used

steps.title: Design & Proposal
steps.subtitle: Technology selection + projected ROI
steps.detail: We evaluate technology alternatives based on the operation's profile. We calculate the 10-year TCO and projected return on investment to select the optimal solution. The proposal includes a 3D layout and flow simulation.
logs: Evaluation of applicable ASRS technologies for the case
10-year TCO and projected ROI calculation with transparent assumptions
Technical and commercial proposal with 3D layout included

steps.title: Installation
steps.subtitle: Detail engineering + certified assembly
steps.detail: We coordinate detailed engineering, mechanical and electrical installation, integration with the existing ERP/WMS and FAT (factory) and SAT (site) acceptance tests before launch.
logs: Mechanical and electrical installation with certified team
ERP/WMS integration for the client (SAP, Oracle and others)
Factory FAT and site SAT before operational launch

steps.title: Commissioning
steps.subtitle: Assisted operational startup
steps.detail: We accompany the startup with direct technical support and real-time parameter adjustment. We are on-site during the first days to ensure a smooth transition and train staff.
logs: On-site support during the first operational days
Parameter adjustment and fine calibration of the system
Final training of operational staff and supervisors

steps.title: Support & Optimization
steps.subtitle: Permanent after-sales service
steps.detail: Remote KPI monitoring, scheduled preventive maintenance and 24/7 local technical support. Continuous analysis of real data to optimize the system and maximize return throughout the equipment's useful life.
logs: Remote KPI monitoring and early alerts 24/7
Scheduled preventive maintenance with local parts
Continuous optimization based on real operational data

catalog.metaTitle: Automation Catalog | DELIE ASRS Systems | STOKA Argentina
catalog.metaDesc: Complete catalog of ASRS systems, robots, vertical storage, transport equipment and DELIE WMS/WCS software for warehouse automation in Argentina.
catalog.heroSub: World-class AS/RS systems, vertical storage, robots and software — with local support in Argentina.

faq.q: Can I combine systems from different categories in the same warehouse?
faq.a: Yes, this is the most common approach in complex projects. For example: unit-load stackers for pallets in mass storage + shuttle robots for high-rotation SKUs + VLM for spare parts, all coordinated by the DELIE WMS. System integration is part of the turnkey project.

faq.q: What is the minimum warehouse size for automation to make sense?
faq.a: There is no square meter threshold. What determines viability is movement volume (generally over 300-500 moves/day), current manual operation cost, and available clear height. STOKA performs the ROI analysis free of charge before any proposal.

faq.q: Are all catalog products available in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. STOKA is the exclusive representative of DELIE in Argentina and Chile. All catalog systems — stackers, shuttle robots, VLM, conveyors and WMS/WCS software — can be installed locally with 100% local engineering, installation and support.

faq.q: Does the catalog include management software or only physical equipment?
faq.a: Both. DELIE has its own WMS (inventory and order management) and WCS (physical equipment control) certified at CMMI Level 5. They integrate with your existing ERP. There are also 3D visualization solutions and an HMS module for AMR robot fleets.

faq.q: Can the system be expanded in the future without stopping operations?
faq.a: Yes, all DELIE systems are modular. A warehouse can start with 2 stacker aisles and scale to 10 without stopping operations. The WMS manages capacity expansion transparently for the existing operation.

faq.q: Does STOKA have a physical presence in Chile?
faq.a: Yes. We have commercial and technical representation in Chile for diagnosis, sales, installation and after-sales support. No need to manage from Argentina.

faq.q: Do DELIE systems comply with Chilean regulations?
faq.a: Yes. The systems are adapted to the industrial safety and construction standards in force in Chile, including specific seismic requirements for permanent installations.

faq.q: What is the delivery time for projects in Chile?
faq.a: It depends on scale. Medium projects (5,000–20,000 positions) have a delivery time of 8–14 months from contract signing, including manufacturing, import and installation.

faq.q: Can an automation project be financed in Chile?
faq.a: Yes. We work with leasing options, bank credit, and in some cases with CORFO and other public agency investment promotion lines.

delie.heroSub: We import, implement and support DELIE ASRS systems in Argentina and Chile. Direct access to the manufacturer with first-class local technical support.

delie.globalPresence: DELIE operates in over 30 countries on 5 continents
delie.whyH2: WHY CHOOSE DELIE WITH LOCAL SUPPORT

whyItems.label: No international intermediaries
whyItems.desc: Buy directly from the exclusive representative. No distributor markups, with factory pricing and direct support.

whyItems.label: Engineering in your language
whyItems.desc: The entire process—diagnosis, engineering, installation and support—in Spanish, with local engineers.

whyItems.label: Parts and spares in stock
whyItems.desc: We maintain critical spare parts inventory in Argentina to minimize downtime in case of any failure.

whyItems.label: Integration with local systems
whyItems.desc: Experience integrating with the most widely used ERPs and accounting systems in Argentina and Chile (SAP, Oracle, Tango, etc.).

delie.tcoText: The total cost of ownership (TCO) of a DELIE system with local support is significantly lower than equivalent European or North American systems, especially considering response times, technician travel costs, and spare parts availability.
delie.comparativaNote: * Based on projects implemented in LATAM. Results may vary by scale and operation type.

faq.q: Is STOKA the only DELIE representative in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. STOKA Group is the exclusive representative and distributor of DELIE for Argentina and Chile. Any DELIE system quotation in these countries must go through STOKA.

faq.q: Do DELIE systems have factory warranty in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. When purchasing through STOKA you get a factory warranty with local backing. We handle any warranty claims directly with DELIE without additional intermediaries.

faq.q: Can I visit a reference installation in Argentina?
faq.a: We have project references in Latin America and Asia available for visits. Depending on the client and their NDA, we coordinate technical visits so you can see the system in real operation.

faq.q: What differentiates DELIE from other Asian brands?
faq.a: DELIE has over 30 years of history, international certifications (CE, ISO) and presence in demanding markets like Europe and Japan. It is not a new brand: it is an established manufacturer with proven track record.

problemas.descripcion: Stock growth exceeds warehouse capacity and expansion is not viable.
problemas.solucion: High-density ASRS systems that multiply capacity without expanding floor area.

problemas.descripcion: Order preparation errors generate returns, losses, and dissatisfied customers.
problemas.solucion: Robots and WMS software with real-time control and full traceability of every movement.

problemas.descripcion: Staff for manual operations represents the largest variable cost of the warehouse.
problemas.solucion: Automation of repetitive movements that frees your team for higher-value tasks.

problemas.descripcion: Without real-time visibility, inventory management depends on slow manual processes.
problemas.solucion: WMS + ERP integration with full visibility from receiving to shipping.

High-volume mass picking with accuracy above 99.9%.
Just-in-time component management for production lines.
SKU storage with high size variability and turnover.

whyItems.desc: Direct access to the manufacturer: no intermediaries, with factory warranty and original parts.

whyItems.label: Local engineering
whyItems.desc: Team of engineers in Argentina and Chile for design, implementation, and after-sales support.

whyItems.label: Local financing
whyItems.desc: We structure each project with financial tools available in the local market.

whyItems.label: Full integration
whyItems.desc: We connect the system to your existing ERP/WMS without interrupting operations.

faq.q: How long does it take to implement an ASRS system?
faq.a: It depends on scale. A medium system (5,000–20,000 positions) generally takes 6–12 months from contract signing to startup. Larger projects may require 18–24 months.

faq.q: Do I need to change my ERP to implement an automated system?
faq.a: No. Our systems integrate with the main ERPs on the market (SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics, etc.) through standard APIs. Integration is part of the project scope.

faq.q: What happens if there's a power outage or technical failure?
faq.a: DELIE systems have emergency operation protocols, UPS, and optional generators. The WMS preserves inventory state and equipment automatically resumes from where they were.

faq.q: Can you finance the project in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. We work with local financing tools: leasing, bank credit, and in some cases access to productive investment promotion lines.

casosDeExito.heroSub: Real DELIE system implementations across different countries and industries, with verified metrics and concrete results.

trustItems.title: Globally proven technology

trustItems.title: Ongoing support

casosDeExito.ctaSub: We evaluate your operation and present a solution with ROI projection at no cost or commitment.

casos.titulo: Automated warehouse for high-volume carpet manufacturer
casos.resumen: A large-scale carpet manufacturer needed to triple storage capacity without expanding floor area. A 4-crane double-mast ASRS with WMS integrated to the existing ERP was implemented.
casos.relevancia: The Argentine textile industry, especially in Córdoba and Buenos Aires, faces the same density challenges. This case demonstrates viability for warehouses of 2,000–8,000 m².

casos.titulo: Automated distribution center for premium spirits warehouse
casos.resumen: Integration of a combined ASRS system (Pallet Shuttle for bulk stock and Miniload for box picking) in a 15,000 m² distribution center. 75% reduction in dispatch times.
casos.relevancia: The wine and beverage sector in Mendoza, San Juan and Cuyo can replicate this model for high-seasonality distribution.

casos.titulo: Automated warehouse for traditional medicine laboratory
casos.resumen: Miniload implementation with batch and expiry control under GMP regulations. Full traceability from raw material intake to finished product dispatch.
casos.relevancia: Argentine pharmaceutical laboratories (ANMAT) require equivalent traceability. The system is adaptable to local regulations without major redesign.

casos.titulo: Cold chain depot for pharmaceutical distribution
casos.resumen: ASRS system specific for 2–8°C storage with real-time temperature control per position. 45% reduction in energy consumption compared to equivalent manual storage.
casos.relevancia: The pharmaceutical cold chain in Argentina is critical. This case directly applies to distributors and laboratories in CABA, Greater Buenos Aires and Córdoba.

casos.titulo: Automated management of bulk industrial chemical raw materials
casos.resumen: Automated storage of raw materials in big-bag and pallet format for continuous production. The system manages automatic FIFO and line replenishment without manual intervention.
casos.relevancia: The petrochemical and chemical industry in Bahía Blanca and Rosario can implement this model for raw material management and production dispatch.

casos.titulo: Automated warehouse for hazardous products under ATEX
casos.resumen: ATEX-certified ASRS system for storage of flammable and toxic products. Includes automatic segregation by hazard class and incompatibility management.
casos.relevancia: Argentine regulations for hazardous materials (IRAM, APN) require similar controls. Chemical, paint and agrochemical industry in Greater Buenos Aires and Córdoba.

casos.titulo: Automated warehouse for titanium and alloy management
casos.resumen: Management of titanium and special alloy profiles, tubes and sheets with traceability by material certificate. Control of environmental conditions and full traceability of each piece.
casos.relevancia: For the Argentine metallurgical industry (Tenaris, etc.) and aerospace suppliers: material traceability with quality certificate.

casos.titulo: Automated distribution center for industrial lubricants
casos.resumen: High-speed distribution of lubricants in multi-format containers (jerry cans, drums, IBC). The system handles 1,200 movements/hour with 99.8% accuracy.
casos.relevancia: Lubricant and petrochemical distributors in Argentina with medium and large-scale operations.

casos.titulo: Central warehouse for large-scale just-in-time manufacturing
casos.resumen: Integrated logistics center supplying 12 production lines just-in-time. The WMS coordinates AGVs, ASRS and conveyors to guarantee components on line with latency below 15 minutes.
casos.relevancia: Industrial parks in Buenos Aires, Córdoba and Rosario with serial production can implement this model to reduce WIP and improve OEE.

alternativaEconomica.metaTitle: Cost-Effective Alternative to European ASRS Systems | STOKA
alternativaEconomica.metaDesc: DELIE ASRS systems with the same technology as European brands but at competitive pricing for Argentina and LATAM. Local financing and technical support
alternativaEconomica.heroSub: DELIE ASRS systems offer the same functionality and reliability as leading European brands, at an acquisition cost up to 40% lower, with local technical support in Argentina.
alternativaEconomica.whyH2: WHY ARE EUROPEAN SYSTEMS MORE EXPENSIVE IN ARGENTINA?

whyCosts.title: Indirect distribution
whyCosts.desc: Many European brands sell through regional distributors with additional margins that increase the final price.

whyCosts.title: Logistics and tariffs
whyCosts.desc: The cost of importing from Europe includes high tariffs, long-term insurance, and more expensive freight.

whyCosts.title: High-cost support
whyCosts.desc: After-sales support involves bringing technicians from Europe or paying local representatives without real system expertise.

alternativaEconomica.comparisonSub: Comparison of technical and commercial features for projects in Argentina and LATAM.
alternativaEconomica.comparisonNote: * Based on comparable projects in LATAM. Results vary by scale and operation type.

comparison.label: Spare parts in stock in Argentina

alternativaEconomica.tcoSub: Not just the purchase price: consider the total cost over 15–20 years of operation. With local support and in-stock spare parts, the TCO of a DELIE system is significantly lower.

tcoStats.desc: Acquisition price vs. equivalent European brands for projects in Argentina
After-sales support cost: local technicians vs. travel from Europe
Additional ROI from local currency financing and available local tax benefits

faq.q: Is DELIE the same quality as European brands like SSI Schäfer or Mecalux?
faq.a: Yes. DELIE has over 30 years of history, CE and ISO certifications, and operates in European and Japanese markets that demand the same standards as traditional European brands. The difference is in cost structure, not technical quality.

faq.q: What about long-term technical support?
faq.a: STOKA maintains a team of DELIE-certified local engineers, with critical spare parts stock in Argentina. After-sales support is guaranteed locally without depending on overseas technicians.

faq.q: Can I see a reference installation before deciding?
faq.a: Yes. We can coordinate technical visits to reference installations in Argentina and Latin America, with prior client agreement. We also have project references in Europe and Asia.

faq.q: Do Argentine tax benefits apply to DELIE systems?
faq.a: Yes. DELIE systems qualify for accelerated depreciation regimes, leasing, and in many cases for capital goods law tax credits. Our team advises on available options in each case.

faq.q: What is the process for getting a quote?
faq.a: The process starts with a free diagnosis of your operation (flows, volumes, SKUs). With that information, we prepare a detailed technical-commercial proposal with projected ROI, at no cost or commitment.

industriaDetail.ctaSub: Our technical team analyzes your operation and proposes the most suitable solution with no commitment.

catalogoAsrs.metaTitle: ASRS Systems Argentina | Automated AS/RS | STOKA
catalogoAsrs.metaDesc: DELIE ASRS and AS/RS in Argentina: stacker cranes, pallet shuttle and miniload from 7 to 40 m height. 30–50% more affordable than European suppliers.
catalogoAsrs.heroSub: DELIE AS/RS racking from 7 to 40 meters tall. Drive-in, shuttle, mezzanine and automated selective rack with local support in Argentina.
catalogoAsrs.whatIsH2: Automated Storage and Retrieval Systems (AS/RS): high-density ASRS
catalogoAsrs.guideTag: Selection guide — Which AS/RS system do you need?

fiscalItems.label: RIMI
fiscalItems.desc: 100% accelerated depreciation in first fiscal year

catalogoAsrs.fiscalRoi: ROI in {{value}} months in mid-scale operations.

faq.q: How much vertical space does an AS/RS system use in my warehouse?
faq.a: DELIE AS/RS systems utilize from 7 to 40 meters of free height. In a warehouse with 12 meters, you can store up to 5 times more than with conventional 4-5 meter racking. The key is designing the structure according to available height and load type.

faq.q: What is the difference between drive-in, shuttle, and MiniLoad racking?
faq.a: Drive-in racking uses conventional forklifts: the most economical, ideal for high-density LIFO. Shuttle racking combines shelving with shuttle robots for higher speed and selective FIFO/LIFO. MiniLoad is for containers and totes with stacker cranes: maximum speed and precision for e-commerce, pharma and high-turnover warehouses.

faq.q: How long does it take to install an AS/RS system in an existing warehouse?
faq.a: Mechanical installation of AS/RS racking takes 4 to 10 weeks depending on the model. Prior civil adaptation (flat slab, lighting) can add 4-6 weeks if the building requires it. STOKA coordinates all detailed engineering, assembly and certified commissioning in Argentina.

faq.q: Does DELIE racking qualify for Argentina's Decree 513/2025 0% import duty?
faq.a: Yes. AS/RS racking for automated systems is included in the tariff positions of Decree 513/2025. STOKA verifies the exact tariff position for each project at no extra cost, ensuring you benefit from the first day of import.

catalogoAsrs.alsoInterest: You may also be interested in
catalogoAsrs.autostoreH3: Evaluated AutoStore? Discover the DELIE alternative
catalogoAsrs.autostoreDesc: Same storage density, 30-50% more affordable and with local technical support in Argentina.

catalogoRobots.heroSub: 14 DELIE warehouse robot models: stacker cranes, MiniLoad stacker cranes, 4-way shuttle robots and tote robots. 24/7 technical support in Argentina.
catalogoRobots.statsH2: Stacker cranes, shuttle robots, AMR, automatic palletizer and goods-to-person automated picking
catalogoRobots.stat1Sub: Most complete range in the market

faq.q: What is the difference between a stacker crane and a shuttle robot in a warehouse?
faq.a: The stacker crane occupies a full racking aisle and moves pallets or totes with a single machine. The shuttle robot travels inside the racking between levels at higher speed and without a dedicated aisle. For warehouses with high turnover and many SKUs, the shuttle generally offers higher throughput per square meter.

faq.q: Can DELIE robots operate in cold storage or refrigerated warehouses?
faq.a: Yes. DELIE manufactures cold chain versions certified to -25°C (shuttle robots) and -30°C (stacker cranes). They include airtight anti-condensation sealing, special lubrication and cold materials. They operate autonomously without staff inside the cold room.

faq.q: How many robots do I need for my operation's throughput?
faq.a: It depends on the required throughput (lines/hour or pallets/hour), SKU count, warehouse layout and demand peaks. During the diagnostic phase, STOKA performs a flow simulation that determines the optimal number of robots to cover peaks without over-sizing the investment.

faq.q: What preventive maintenance do warehouse robots require?
faq.a: DELIE robots require 2-4 hours of preventive maintenance per month per unit. The WCS monitors each robot's status in real time and generates early alerts. High-wear components are modular and replaced without specialized tools. STOKA maintains local spare parts stock in Argentina.

catalogoRobots.metaTitle: Handling Robots | Stacker Cranes & AMR | STOKA
catalogoRobots.metaDesc: DELIE warehouse robot catalog: stacker cranes, MiniLoad cranes, shuttle robots and AMR. 14 models with local STOKA support in Argentina and Chile.

catalogoVertical.heroSub: Save up to 90% of floor space. Ergonomic "goods-to-person" picking at operator height, no walking. Integration with ERP and MES.
catalogoVertical.statsH2: Automated vertical warehouse: VLM, carousel and storage tower to optimize space

techItems.name: VLM — Vertical Lift Module
techItems.desc: Smart extractor that retrieves any tray independently. Ideal when there is high SKU variety and the operator needs different products each cycle.
techItems.best: Pharma · Spare parts · E-commerce with many SKUs

techItems.name: Intelligent vertical carousel
techItems.desc: System that circulates trays internally. More efficient for repetitive high-turnover SKUs where sequential access is adequate.

catalogoVertical.faqH2: Frequently asked questions about vertical storage

faq.q: When is a VLM better and when is a vertical carousel better for my warehouse?
faq.a: The VLM is best when you have high SKU variety and need random access: each cycle you can retrieve any tray independently. The carousel is more efficient for high-turnover SKUs with sequential access. For pharma, spare parts and e-commerce with many different SKUs, the VLM is generally the best option.

faq.q: How much floor space do I save with a VLM in my warehouse?
faq.a: Typically between 70% and 90% compared to conventional shelving. A 10-meter VLM occupies 5-8 m² of floor area and stores the equivalent of 30-50 m² of conventional shelving. In buildings with free height, storage density per floor m² multiplies between 4 and 8 times.

faq.q: How does the VLM integrate with my existing ERP or WMS?
faq.a: Via standard RESTful APIs or DELIE's WMS interface. If you already have a third-party WMS (SAP EWM, Manhattan, etc.), the VLM can operate as a slave subsystem that receives extraction orders. If you don't have a WMS, DELIE's module manages warehouse inventory autonomously with ERP integration.

faq.q: Can the VLM or vertical carousel operate in temperature-controlled environments?
faq.a: The vertical carousel operates between -5°C and +40°C, compatible with positive cold rooms. The VLM has versions for environments between 2°C and 25°C, ideal for pharma and refrigerated products. For negative cold (frozen storage), DELIE's specialized AS/RS stacker cranes are recommended.

catalogoVertical.metaDesc: DELIE vertical storage systems: VLM and smart carousels. Save up to 90% floor space with goods-to-person picking. Local STOKA support in Argentina.

catalogoTransporte.heroSub: 10 transport equipment models to connect every point of your automated warehouse. From chain conveyors to automatic palletizers with WCS integration.
catalogoTransporte.statsH2: The connective tissue of the automated warehouse — conveyors, lifters and palletizers integrated under WCS

faq.q: What type of conveyor do I need for 3,000 kg pallets in my warehouse?
faq.a: For 3,000 kg pallet loads, DELIE's chain or roller conveyor is the right choice. Both are sized for that load with a frequency inverter for smooth start. The choice between chain and rollers depends on pallet fragility and whether you need zone accumulation in the warehouse.

faq.q: Can I integrate existing conveyors in my warehouse with the DELIE system?
faq.a: In many cases, yes. DELIE's WCS can control conveyors from other brands with standard control interfaces (PLC with Profibus, Profinet or similar). STOKA performs a compatibility audit during the technical diagnosis to determine which existing conveyors can be reused to reduce total investment.

faq.q: What maintenance do DELIE conveyors require?
faq.a: DELIE conveyors have quarterly preventive maintenance: chain or roller lubrication, tensioner verification and sensor check. Wear components are standardized and available in Argentina. The WCS monitors each warehouse section and issues alerts before a failure occurs.

faq.q: Do DELIE conveyors work at negative temperatures in cold rooms?
faq.a: Yes. Cold chain conveyors operate down to -25°C with special lubricants, anti-freeze materials and hermetic design. They integrate with DELIE's cold line stacker cranes and shuttle robots to create a fully automated cold storage system.

catalogoTransporte.metaDesc: DELIE conveying catalog: conveyors, lifts, palletizers and transfer machines. 10 models with local STOKA engineering in Argentina and Chile.

catalogoSoftware.heroSub: 6 DELIE software modules CMMI Level 5 certified. Full control of the automated warehouse with SAP, Oracle and any ERP integration. 3D Digital Twin included.
catalogoSoftware.seoH2: WMS vs WCS: differences and complementarity
catalogoSoftware.benefitsTag: Key advantages of the DELIE stack
catalogoSoftware.errorTag: The most common mistake in AS/RS projects
catalogoSoftware.errorH3: Implementing hardware without designing the software layer.
catalogoSoftware.errorBody: A stacker crane without adequate WCS operates as a manual machine: efficient in physical movement but without the intelligence to coordinate combined cycles, manage real-time inventory and integrate with the ERP. DELIE's WMS and WCS were designed as an integrated stack: same database, same interface, same source of truth for warehouse inventory.

comparison.role: Manages WHAT: inventory, locations, orders, traceability.
Manages HOW: coordinates robots, stacker cranes and conveyors in real time.
comparison.integra: WMS, robots, stacker cranes, AMR, RGV

comparison.role: Interface for operators and supervisors on the floor.
Electrical control of motors, drives and sensors.

faq.q: How long does it take to implement the WMS in my warehouse?
faq.a: Base WMS implementation takes between 4 and 12 weeks depending on ERP integration complexity. In projects with DELIE hardware, the WMS is configured simultaneously with mechanical installation, not adding time to the overall schedule. STOKA manages software implementation as part of the turnkey project.

faq.q: Can the WMS manage a manual warehouse (without robots)?
faq.a: Yes. The WMS can be deployed in a manual warehouse as a first digitization step, before automating. It manages real-time inventory, list-based or pick-to-light picking, FIFO/FEFO and shift reports. This approach lets the team learn the system before adding automated hardware.

faq.q: How does the technical WMS integration with SAP or ERP work?
faq.a: DELIE has native connectors for SAP (BAPI/RFC and SAP EWM), Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics and RESTful APIs for any other system. Integration includes item and location master synchronization, inbound/outbound orders and real-time confirmations. STOKA delivers complete technical documentation.

faq.q: What happens to warehouse data if the WMS server goes down?
faq.a: The WMS has active-passive redundancy: if the primary server fails, the backup takes control in seconds with no data loss. Robots and conveyors have a short-term autonomous operation mode. All movements are recorded with timestamps and automatically synced when the system recovers.

catalogoSoftware.metaTitle: Warehouse Software | WMS, WCS & Control | STOKA
catalogoSoftware.metaDesc: CMMI Level 5 certified DELIE software: WMS, WCS, HMS and automated warehouse control. 6 integrated modules with local STOKA support in Argentina.

autoStoreAlternativa.faqH2: Frequently asked questions about AutoStore alternatives in Argentina

faq.q: Is DELIE an alternative to AutoStore in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. DELIE manufactures ASRS cube storage, shuttle and stacker crane systems that compete directly with AutoStore. The main difference is price: DELIE systems are 30-50% more affordable than AutoStore for equivalent projects, with local technical support in Argentina through STOKA.

faq.q: What is the technical difference between AutoStore and DELIE systems?
faq.a: AutoStore uses grid robots operating on a modular grid. DELIE offers shuttle and stacker crane systems that in many configurations achieve comparable or superior densities, especially in warehouses with more than 8 meters of available height. DELIE also handles larger and heavier SKUs.

faq.q: How much does an AutoStore alternative system cost in Argentina?
faq.a: A DELIE system equivalent to a medium AutoStore installation (1,000–3,000 positions) in Argentina can cost between USD 600,000 and USD 1,500,000 complete (equipment + racking + WCS + integration). AutoStore typically costs 30-50% more for equivalent configurations, plus the cost of imported technical support.

faq.q: Does STOKA have experience installing systems in operations similar to AutoStore?
faq.a: Yes. STOKA has installed DELIE systems in e-commerce, 3PL logistics, pharma and manufacturing operations in Argentina with throughput profiles similar to those typically automated with AutoStore. We can show sector references at the first technical meeting.

beneficiosFiscales.metaDesc: RIMI 2026, RIGI, accelerated depreciation and early VAT recovery: up to 6 tax benefits to reduce the cost of your ASRS in Argentina. Updated guide 2026.
beneficiosFiscales.heroSub: Argentina has at least 6 fiscal tools that can reduce the real cost of your ASRS system by 15% to 40%. It's not just RIGI — there are options from USD 150,000 in investment.
beneficiosFiscales.mapaDisclaimer: * This table is indicative. The applicability of each tool depends on the company's tax profile and project structure.

mapaRows.beneficio: Accelerated depreciation in 2 years + VAT refund
Income tax at 25% + full import tariff exemption
Exemptions on IIBB, Property and Stamp taxes

beneficiosFiscales.rimiIntro: The Manufacturing and Industry Incentive Regime (RIMI), created by Ley 27.802 Title XXIII and regulated by Decreto 242/2026 and Joint General Resolution ARCA 5849/2026, allows Argentine SMEs to depreciate capital goods in 2 years (instead of their accounting useful life) and recover paid VAT in advance. It applies to companies holding a valid MiPyME certificate.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiCalificaP: Yes. ASRS systems —stacker cranes, shuttles, miniloads, carousels and palletizing robots— qualify as Capital Goods (BK) or Technological Infrastructure Goods (BIT) under the RIMI classification. Warehouse management software (WMS) and the warehouse control system (WCS) qualify as BIT when acquired as an integral part of the project.

rimiCalificaItems.label: WMS / WCS integrated into the project

beneficiosFiscales.rimiB1H3: Benefit 1 — Accelerated depreciation in Income Tax
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB1P: Instead of depreciating the asset over its accounting useful life (typically 5 to 10 years), RIMI allows deducting 100% of the acquisition value in 2 equal annual installments of Income Tax. The actual saving depends on the rate applicable to the company.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB1Ej2: USD 1,000,000 is deducted in the first fiscal year (35% rate → ~USD 350,000 saving in Income Tax).
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB1Ej3: deduction of USD 200,000/year → ~USD 70,000 saving in Income Tax over the same period.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB2P: The VAT paid on the import or local purchase of capital goods can be recovered within 9 months: ARCA/AFIP has 3 months to process the request and 6 additional months to make the refund. The credit can be applied against other national taxes, collected in cash or transferred to third parties.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB2Ej1: Import of an ASRS system for USD 2,000,000.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB2Ej2: VAT at 21% on CIF value: ≈ USD 420,000 recoverable in ≤9 months.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB2Ej3: Without RIMI, that tax credit is gradually absorbed against future debits, immobilizing working capital for years.

rimiLimits.title: Budget quota
rimiLimits.desc: RIMI operates with an annual quota assigned by ARCA. 50% of the quota is reserved for projects approved in previous rounds; the remaining 50% is assigned by order of submission. We recommend starting the process in the first quarter of the fiscal year.

rimiLimits.title: SGI (Incentive Management System)
rimiLimits.desc: The filing platform may have closing or maintenance periods. Check the current opening window on the ARCA website before starting the documentation.

rimiLimits.title: Equity seniority
rimiLimits.desc: The company must prove at least 2 years of activity with positive net worth at the time of submission. Recently incorporated companies may not qualify.

beneficiosFiscales.rimiLegal: Regulations: Ley 27.802 Title XXIII · Decreto 242/2026 · Joint GR ARCA 5849/2026. Applicability depends on the tax profile and project structure. Consult your accountant.
beneficiosFiscales.rigiH2: RIGI — If your project exceeds USD 200 million
beneficiosFiscales.rigiIntro: The Large Investment Incentive Regime (RIGI), created by Ley 27.742 and regulated by Decreto 749/2024, is designed for strategic projects of USD 200M or more in mining, oil and gas, energy, technology and infrastructure. A typical ASRS system (USD 500K–5M) does not reach that threshold on its own — but if your company already has a project enrolled in RIGI, the warehouse automation component can be included as part of the approved promoted investment.
beneficiosFiscales.rigiIntro2: To date, 27 projects worth over USD 25,000M have been submitted. The regime was extended until July 2027. Enrolled provinces include: Mendoza, San Juan, Neuquén, Salta, Río Negro, Chubut, Santa Cruz, CABA, Córdoba and Buenos Aires, among others.
beneficiosFiscales.rigiKeyP: If your company has a project enrolled in RIGI, your ASRS system is part of the promoted investment package. We help you structure the technical documentation of the automation component so it is correctly included in the approved file.
beneficiosFiscales.rigiLegal: Regulations: Ley 27.742 · Decreto 749/2024. Enrollment in RIGI is voluntary and entails minimum investment commitments. Verify eligibility with a specialized legal advisor.
beneficiosFiscales.arancelH2: Reduced import tariffs for capital goods
beneficiosFiscales.arancelIntro: Decreto 513/2025 established the tariff reduction for 27 categories of capital goods, including logistics and industrial automation systems. The general tariff for new imported machinery ranges between 0% and 14%, depending on the NCM classification of the component.
beneficiosFiscales.arancelKeyP: There are no Argentine manufacturers of stacker cranes or ASRS systems. This enables the "capital good not produced locally" argument to classify the equipment under the NCM position with a 0% tariff, provided the classification is validated by the customs broker before importation.
beneficiosFiscales.arancelWarn: The exact tariff depends on the NCM classification of each component. We help you verify it with your customs broker before importation to maximize the benefit.
beneficiosFiscales.arancelLegal: Regulations: Decreto 513/2025 (National Executive Branch). Validity subject to annual review. Verify NCM classification with an authorized broker.
beneficiosFiscales.ivaH2: VAT reduced to 10.5% for capital goods
beneficiosFiscales.ivaIntro: The VAT Law (art. 28, Ley 23.966) sets a differential rate of 10.5% —instead of the general 21%— for capital goods included in the specific Executive Branch list. Applicability depends on the NCM tariff classification of the equipment imported or acquired locally.
beneficiosFiscales.ivaWarn: Does not apply if the operation is structured via leasing. When the acquisition is channeled through a leasing contract, the VAT on installments is settled at the general 21% rate.
beneficiosFiscales.ivaLegal: Regulations: Ley 23.966 art. 28 · Annex of capital goods with reduced rate. Applicability depends on the NCM classification of the equipment.
beneficiosFiscales.leasingH2: Capital goods leasing: finance without immobilizing capital
beneficiosFiscales.leasingIntro: Financial leasing (Ley 25.248) allows acquiring an ASRS system financing 100% of the asset value, including VAT, through periodic installments. It is the alternative for companies that prefer to preserve liquidity and not commit conventional credit lines.

leasingCards.title: 100% financing
leasingCards.desc: Covers the full asset value plus VAT. No mandatory down payment or initial payment.

leasingCards.title: Deductible installments
leasingCards.desc: Each installment is deducted as an Income Tax expense, generating tax savings from the first month.

leasingCards.title: Deferred VAT
leasingCards.desc: VAT is split across the installments, avoiding a concentrated outlay at the start.

leasingCards.title: No accounting debt
leasingCards.desc: The asset is not recorded as debt on the balance sheet until the purchase option is exercised.

leasingCards.title: Technological flexibility
leasingCards.desc: Option to return or renew the equipment if technology evolves before the contract ends.

leasingCards.title: De facto depreciation
leasingCards.desc: The installment scheme replicates the effect of accelerated depreciation on cash flow.

leasingEnt.detalle: Fixed rate from 19.45% per year in USD

beneficiosFiscales.leasingWarn: RIMI requires keeping the asset in the company's equity for at least 2 years from the investment. In leasing, legal ownership of the asset belongs to the bank until the purchase option is exercised. Consult your tax advisor on the optimal structure before combining both regimes.
beneficiosFiscales.leasingLegal: Regulations: Ley 25.248 (Leasing). Rates and terms are indicative and subject to update by each entity.
beneficiosFiscales.ecH2: Knowledge Economy: tax benefit when buying from us
beneficiosFiscales.ecIntro: STOKA, as an industrial automation technology integrator (Industry 4.0), is in the process of registering in the Knowledge Economy Regime (Ley 27.506). Once registered, companies that acquire digital transformation and Industry 4.0 solutions from certified providers can access FONPEC: a tax credit credited directly in ARCA, under Resolution 8/2026.
beneficiosFiscales.ecFonpecP: The Knowledge Economy Promotion Fund (FONPEC) is a tax credit equivalent to a percentage of the contract, credited to the client's ARCA account. It can be applied against national taxes: Income Tax, VAT, employer contributions. It is a benefit received by the buyer for contracting with a registered provider.
beneficiosFiscales.ecWhyTag: Why it matters for your purchase decision
ecWhyItems: Buying from a provider registered in the Knowledge Economy generates extra tax credit for your company
Resolution 8/2026: instrument to promote investment in Industry 4.0
The same project can combine FONPEC + RIMI + Decreto 513
Differentiator: registered KE provider vs. non-registered = different real cost
beneficiosFiscales.ecLegal: Regulations: Ley 27.506 (Knowledge Economy) · FONPEC Resolution 8/2026. Status: STOKA registration in progress. Check the benefit's validity at the time of the operation.
beneficiosFiscales.provIntro: Provincial benefits are complementary to the national ones (RIMI, RIGI, tariffs). The optimal combination depends on the province where your company operates. We help you map the incentives available for your case.

beneficiosFiscales.provMineLink: Operating in mining or oil & gas? See specific benefits for the mining-energy sector →
beneficiosFiscales.biceDesc: BICE offers a specific line for modernization and automation projects of productive and logistics processes. With terms of up to 10 years and a 2-year grace period, it is the most convenient financing instrument in the Argentine market for productive capital goods investment projects.
biceItems: Financing of up to 80% of the total project value
Terms of up to 10 years for industrial automation projects
Grace period of up to 2 years on principal
Preferential interest rate based on the project's credit profile
Applicable to SMEs and large companies via an enrolled commercial bank
STOKA supports the preparation of the technical project required by the bank
Compatible with the 0% tariff of Decreto 513/2025 and RIMI in the same operation
beneficiosFiscales.pymeIntro: Beyond the investment regimes, SMEs with a valid MiPyME certificate access a series of operational benefits that improve cash flow during and after importation.

pymeCards.title: VAT creditable at 90 days
pymeCards.desc: The tax credit for VAT paid on importation can be computed at 90 days, improving the cash position in the quarter of the operation.

pymeCards.title: 20% advance on capital goods imports
pymeCards.desc: Advance payment reduced to 20% of the asset value over the total import, instead of the standard percentage.

pymeCards.title: Simplified VAT non-withholding certificate
pymeCards.desc: Simplified process to obtain the certificate that avoids withholding at source, reducing administrative work.

pymeCards.title: Exemption from import perceptions
pymeCards.desc: Temporary exemption from VAT and IIBB perceptions on imports for qualified SMEs. Verify validity with ARCA at the time of the operation.

pymeCards.title: Check tax as payment on account
pymeCards.desc: Micro companies can compute 30% of the Bank Debits and Credits Tax as payment on account of employer contributions, reducing the monthly labor burden.

beneficiosFiscales.aplicaH2: Which benefits apply to your company?
beneficiosFiscales.aplicaNote: — = does not apply directly · ✅ = applies · The optimal combination depends on the tax profile, corporate structure and location of the company.
beneficiosFiscales.casoH2: How much does the real cost drop with all benefits combined?

casoCards.label: Project value
casoCards.desc: Complete ASRS system: stacker cranes, shuttle robots, WMS and conveyors for a distribution warehouse.

casoCards.label: 0% tariff saving
casoCards.desc: Decreto 513/2025 eliminates the 15% tariff on CIF value. Direct saving in the initial outlay.

casoCards.label: VAT 10.5% vs 21%
casoCards.desc: Differential VAT on the equipment's taxable base (saving of 10.5 percentage points on USD 500,000 of qualified components).

casoCards.label: BICE financing
casoCards.desc: 80% of the project over 10 years. Estimated installment: USD 8,500/month, lower than the monthly labor cost replaced.

beneficiosFiscales.casoResH3: The result on first-year cash flow
beneficiosFiscales.casoResP1: With a 0% tariff and 80% BICE financing, the company disburses USD 50,000 of its own capital in the first year (USD 200,000 net contribution from the USD 150,000 tariff saving). The monthly BICE installments are lower than the labor cost the system replaces.
beneficiosFiscales.casoResP2: On that scenario, RIMI allows deducting 100% of the asset value in the first fiscal year, reducing the Income Tax base for the year of the investment. In companies with a high tax burden, this benefit can equal an additional tax saving of USD 80,000 to USD 120,000.
beneficiosFiscales.casoNote: * Values are indicative estimates. The actual benefit depends on the company's tax profile, the project structure and the regimes it enrolls in. STOKA analyzes each specific project free of charge.
beneficiosFiscales.ctx2026H2: Why 2026 is a window of opportunity to automate
ctx2026P: The convergence of three factors makes 2025–2026 a unique window to invest in warehouse automation in Argentina. For the first time in decades, long-term regulatory stability, elimination of import tariffs and access to preferential financing combine simultaneously.

RIGI offers 30 years of tax stability: Income Tax at 25%, fixed tariffs and free availability of foreign currency from the third year. For companies automating today, this horizon of certainty makes it easier to calculate the 10-year TCO and present the project to the board or financiers.

Decreto 513/2025 eliminates the 15% tariff: on a USD 800,000 project, that is USD 120,000 less in initial outlay. With no additional management by the company: it applies automatically when importing DELIE equipment.

The combination of BICE + RIMI + 0% tariff transforms the investment equation: the monthly financing installment falls below the monthly labor cost being replaced, making the system pay for itself from the first year of operation.

faqItems.q: Does an imported ASRS system qualify as a capital good for RIMI?
faqItems.a: Yes. Decreto 242/2026 includes new imported movable goods classified as Capital Goods (BK). An ASRS system (stacker cranes, robots, conveyors) fits this definition. WMS/WCS software could be classified as an Informatics and Telecommunications Good (BIT).

faqItems.q: How much can I really save?
faqItems.a: It depends on the amount, your company's tax structure and the applicable regimes. As a reference: on a USD 2M project, RIMI's accelerated depreciation can represent an estimated financial saving of USD 280,000 in the first 2 years, and the reduced VAT rate a direct saving of USD 210,000 if the 10.5% rate applies.

faqItems.q: Does STOKA handle the tax procedure?
faqItems.a: No. STOKA supports with the equipment's technical documentation (datasheets, NCM classifications, capital good classification). Enrollment in RIMI/RIGI and the tax procedures are handled by your accountant or tax advisor.

It is a legal gray area. RIMI requires the asset to be in the beneficiary's equity for 2 years; in leasing, ownership belongs to the bank. We recommend consulting your tax advisor to define the optimal structure.

faqItems.q: What if my company is not an SME?
faqItems.a: If you are not an SME and your project does not reach USD 200M (RIGI), you can still take advantage of: reduced tariffs, VAT 10.5% (if the NCM qualifies), leasing and provincial benefits depending on your location.

faqItems.q: Do RIGI benefits apply to the ASRS within a mining or oil project?
faqItems.a: Yes. If your company has a project enrolled in RIGI, the project's capital goods (including an ASRS) enjoy full exemption from import duties, VAT with tax credit certificates and accelerated depreciation.

beneficiosFiscales.ctaP: Every company has a different tax structure. Our team supports you with the technical documentation of the ASRS system and connects you with the benefits that apply to your case. The final tax advice is defined by your accountant — we provide the engineering.
beneficiosFiscales.disclaimerP: The information on this page is general in nature and based on regulations in force as of June 2026. It does not constitute tax, legal or financial advice. Tax benefits are subject to current regulations, budget quotas and eligibility requirements that may vary. Consult your accountant or tax advisor on the applicability to your particular case. Last updated: June 2026. STOKA is not responsible for decisions made based on this information without independent professional advice.
